title: aglaia roxburghiana var. pauperfula
intro: Aglaia roxburghiana var. pauperfula, also known as the Pauperia aglaia or Roxburgh's aglaia, is a plant species that belongs to the family Meliaceae. It is traditionally used in various parts of the world for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Aglaia roxburghiana var. pauperfula include reducing fever, alleviating pain and inflammation, and providing antioxidant and immunomodulatory effects. Herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which are often used to treat various health conditions and as a natural remedy for colds and flu.
